div1.entryFree:
Verbal, 1) literal, having word answering to word: all the neighbour caves . . . make v. repetition of her moans, Ven. 831.
2) spoken, not written: she told me in a sweet v. brief, All's V, 3, 137.
3) expressed or conveyed in words: made she no v. question? Lr. IV, 3, 26 (== did she not speak?).
4) plain-spoken, wording one's thoughts without reserve: you put me to forget a lady's manners, by being so v. Cymb. II, 3, 111 (according to others, == verbose).
